source.name: Meaning Alignment Institute — Moral Graph (the first moral graph)
source.study: Moral Graph Elicitation with a representative sample of ~500 US participants on 3 deliberately divisive prompts (abortion, parenting, diverse viewpoints).
source.citation: Klingefjord, Lowe & Edelman, "What are human values, and how do we align AI to them?", arXiv:2404.10636 (2024).

access.tool_repository: https://github.com/meaningalignment/moral-graph-elicitation (generator tool; not used for this import)

revision.note: The export is a static JSON endpoint with no version-control revision; the download is anchored by sha256 and date.

license.detail: No LICENSE file in either MAI repository (GitHub `license: null`); the data is published openly but states no reuse terms (not MIT, CC-BY, or similar).

usage.publication: Do not publish MAI-derived data until cleared with Joe Edelman (MAI). Raw and normalized data are gitignored; only this manifest and scripts/values.py are committed.

value_cards.description: Attention policies. May be retrieved by semantic relevance.

moral_graph_edges.description: Contextual, human-voted 'wiser than' judgments. Looked up only by value id; never inferred from card similarity.

firewall: Value cards and moral-graph edges are stored in separate files. The application must not infer edges from card similarity. See knowledge/values/README.md.
